text.title: Choosing and evaluating a reranker
text.summary: Which reranker fits which workload, the eval harness that justifies the pick, and the refresh cadence that keeps it honest.

list.item: Hosted API (Cohere Rerank 3, Voyage rerank-2.5) — strongest out of the box, opaque pricing, vendor lock-in. ~50-150 ms per 100 candidates.
Open weights, small (BGE-reranker-base, jina-reranker-v2-base) — self-hostable on CPU; ~5-15 pp behind hosted on hard suites; cheapest at scale.
Open weights, large (BGE-reranker-large, e5-mistral-instruct) — closes the gap to hosted on most suites; needs a GPU or strong CPU.
LLM-as-reranker (Haiku scoring relevance) — flexible, no infra; expensive at high throughput; useful as a baseline to evaluate cheaper rerankers.

rule.statement: Pick a reranker by running an eval suite drawn from your real traffic, not by reading a public leaderboard alone; public benchmarks systematically over-rank hosted rerankers on academic corpora that do not match enterprise traffic.
rule.rationale: ZeroEntropy 2025 reports rank flips between BGE-large and Cohere Rerank 3 across domains; per-domain eval is the only reliable signal.

example.code: # Eval harness — run all four candidate rerankers on the same labelled set for r in [cohere_v3, bge_large, voyage_2_5, jina_v2]: score = ndcg_at_10(r.rank(queries, candidates), labels) cost = r.price_per_call * len(queries) log({"reranker": r.name, "ndcg@10": score, "cost_usd": cost})
example.note: Plot quality vs cost; pick the Pareto-front reranker for your latency budget.

section.title: Refresh cadence
section.p: Reranker quality drifts with corpus drift, not with model age. Re-evaluate on a freshly labelled suite when the corpus has shifted significantly or when a new reranker version ships.

rule.statement: Re-run the reranker eval whenever (a) the corpus has rotated more than 20% of chunks, (b) the embedding model is retrained, or (c) the reranker vendor ships a new version; do not re-evaluate on a fixed cadence.
rule.rationale: Calendar-based evals waste effort during stable periods and miss real shifts; corpus-rotation triggers track the actual signal.

antipattern.description: Bolting a hosted reranker onto a single-stage pipeline without an eval harness.
antipattern.reason: Without measurement, the reranker may be making things worse on your data — public-benchmark leaders sometimes lose on niche corpora.

section.reference: Anthropic Contextual Retrieval — reranker as the precision layer.
